title: Near miss - scaffolding board dislodged at Level 4
description: A 2x10 scaffold board became dislodged at Level 4 east concourse during shift change and fell 22 feet to Level 3 barricaded zone below. No workers were in the fall zone. Cause: board was not toe-boarded and was displaced by equipment movement. Immediate corrective action: all scaffold boards on project re-inspected and toe-boarded within 4 hours. Revised scaffold inspection protocol implemented. All scaffold foremen retrained.

title: First aid - wire tie laceration right hand
description: Ironworker laborer sustained minor laceration to right palm while handling wire tie wire without gloves. Wound cleaned and bandaged at site medical trailer. Worker returned to full duty. Root cause: worker removed cut-resistant gloves while tying rebar. Corrective action: glove removal policy reinforced with crew at next day safety meeting.

title: First aid - concrete dust eye irritation
description: Subcontractor worker sustained eye irritation from concrete dust during Level 1 slab grinding operations. Eyes flushed at eyewash station for 15 minutes. Treated at medical trailer and released. No vision impairment. Root cause: worker not wearing full seal safety glasses during grinding operations. Corrective action: mandatory full seal goggles for all grinding operations.

title: Near miss - personnel too close to crane swing radius
description: During TC-1 pick on roof truss section 6 a subcontractor worker was observed inside the crane swing radius exclusion zone while load was moving. Crane operator spotted worker and held load. Safety director immediately escorted worker from zone and issued written warning. Root cause: worker entered zone via unmarked gap in barricade. Corrective action: continuous barricade installed with no gaps and exclusion zone spotters assigned to all picks.

title: OSHA Recordable - twisted ankle elevated work surface
description: Ironworker twisted right ankle when stepping from steel erection platform to beam flange at Level 3 northwest quadrant. Worker felt pop and was unable to return to work. Transported to WorkCare clinic. Diagnosis: Grade 2 ankle sprain. Work restriction: no climbing for 21 days reassigned to ground level duties. OSHA 300 log entry filed. Toolbox talk on step transitions conducted following day.

title: Property damage - concrete pump hose failure
description: Schwing stationary pump hose failed at coupler during Level 4 east deck pour resulting in concrete blowout. Approximately 8 CY of concrete discharged onto Level 3 below which was unoccupied barricaded area. No injuries. Approximately 4 hours of cleanup and hose replacement. Pour resumed same day. Investigation: hose was past inspection interval and should have been replaced. New hose inspection and replacement protocol implemented.
